title: Variational Eigensolver Lab
description: Implement a small VQE-style workflow that uses a parameterised circuit and a classical optimiser to minimise a simple Hamiltonian.
whyItMatters: Variational methods are one of the most important near-term quantum algorithm patterns because they embrace noisy hardware and hybrid control loops.

steps.title: Choose a simple Hamiltonian
steps.description: Select a small problem such as a toy two-level system or a minimal chemistry example.
steps.tips: The educational goal is the loop, not maximum physical realism.

steps.title: Build an ansatz circuit
steps.description: Create a parameterised quantum circuit with a small number of tunable angles.
steps.tips: A compact ansatz makes the optimisation easier to debug.

steps.title: Estimate the cost function
steps.description: Measure the expectation value of the Hamiltonian for each parameter choice.
steps.tips: Track the objective history so you can see optimisation behaviour.

steps.title: Close the classical loop
steps.description: Use a classical optimiser to update the ansatz parameters and repeat.
steps.tips: Expect local minima and noisy objective estimates.

steps.title: Review the outcome
steps.description: Explain what the algorithm found, what limited it, and whether the result reflects a real improvement.
steps.tips: The interpretation matters as much as the final number.

deliverables: Short reflection on optimisation quality and hardware realism

rubric.description: The hybrid quantum-classical cycle is implemented correctly.
The learner can explain what the optimiser is doing and where it can fail.
The final output reflects more than just copied boilerplate.
